Some sad news that I hate to share...

A brilliant, sunshine-of-a-person Sajjad, from the very first cohort of School for Everyday Democracy, has had a brain stem stroke while visiting family in Pakistan. He lights up a room just by being in it, and anyone who knows him will know what a wonderful, kind, and full-of-life character he is.

Right now, his family are working to get him back home to the UK safely, and they’ve set up a GoFundMe to help cover the costs. If you can give, or even just share the page to spread the word, it would be amazing ✨

From his family: 'My dad has spent his life giving to others by volunteering at the TRA with the council and community events by running the Desi food stall in Swiss Cottage, organizing charity Christmas boxes, contributing to community theatre in Hampstead. He loved sharing his love of classic novels at St John’s Wood library. He is a loving husband and devoted father to five children Halima (23), Qasim (19), Hajra and Saira (17), and Muhammad (12).'.

content:
Post 1: August 2024 — When Building Our Own LLM Made Sense

In August 2024, at Adya, we built our first fine-tuned large language model.
At that time, it felt like the obvious thing to do.

 GPT-4o was the benchmark. Open-source models were just arriving. Fine-tuning looked like the future.

By September, our model was showing ~21% better efficiency than leading models for retail tasks, and used ~85% fewer tokens.

On paper, it looked like a moat.

But that moment didn’t last.

Because what we learned over the next few months changed how we think about AI forever.

This is the story of why we stopped chasing “the best model” — and started building a platform instead.

👇 More in the next post.

#AI #GenAI #Startups #LLMs #Adya

content:
🧠 The AI Shift #5 is live! 

We break down the post budget sentiments here.

In this latest deep dive for Inc42 Media, I unpack how AI is being embedded across governance, public platforms, and core digital infrastructure, with signals that stretch decades, not quarters.

For the government, AI is now a force multiplier. For investors, it’s a long-duration infrastructure bet. For startups, it’s a more complicated story.

The budget pushes compute, data centres, and sovereign AI capacity hard. Tax holidays till 2047. Safe harbour provisions. Mission-mode execution. All signs of long-term intent.

This edition dives into:

- Why AI is being treated as public infrastructure, not a venture theme
- What the data centre and compute push really signals to capital
- Why startup upside may remain indirect, not policy-led
- How investors are shifting focus from models to execution and domain depth

You’ll find perspectives from: Founders, policymakers, and investors across India’s AI ecosystem, including a post budget comment from Abhishek Singh (CEO, IndiaAI Mission).

And, Ankush Sabharwal (CoRover.ai), Raj K Gopalakrishnan (KOGO AI), Pranav Pai (3one4 Capital), and Shayak Mazumder (Adya.ai)

content:
Well begun is half-done, they say.

 And a great start was exactly what we were off to at KCDH, IIT, Bombay, with a meeting of brilliant minds to address one the most important issues in healthcare in India today- the stark underrepresentation of the Indian Genome in global research.


 With a highly powered team from across the spectrum of interest groups represented by IGIB, Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay , Premas Life Sciences Pvt Ltd. Adya , HCG Hospitals  and Colossa Ventures,  the day saw a free flow of ideas, insights and perspectives foundational to our vision of an AI-ready Data commons for Genomic and other Omic patient data.

This is the beginning of a concerted effort to create a unique, national level framework and ecosystem that will fuel innovation, collaboration and monetization across Research, Industry, Healthcare and Investors, with the ultimate goal of precision medicine and improved outcomes for the Indian Patient.

 I ended the day with unadulterated optimism for our field- feeling deeply energized by thought leaders and experts including Praveen K. Gupta, Debjani Saha, Shayak Mazumder,Chinmaya G. D. Suryavamse ,Ashish Mittal , Dr Richa Singh, Vandana Rajadhyaksha,  Uday Chatterjee, and  Shantanu Chowdhury at Koita Centre for Digital Health - KCDH (IIT Bombay), Ranjith Padinhateeri , Kshitij Jadhav , Nirmal Punjabi, Ph.D. Saket Choudhary

Thank you, Shantanu Chowdhury for the vision and Ranjith Padinhateeri for making it happen. Looking forward to making this a reality!

content:
Why Adya Launch Lab  or ALL AI? I was presenting to 25 CXOs in New York when someone asked me how we were creating such a kick ass AI company from India. 

That hit a raw nerve. We used to see China as the producer of low quality products produced en masse. Now, the world is starting to see India as the producer of low quality engineers en masse.

We missed the internet revolution and the cloud as well. Are we about to miss the AI revolution? Why is India #12 in AI globally, when it is #2 in the no of engineers?

Will all our engineers need to go to the US to find value? Even if they do, with rising anti-India sentiments in the US, how long will we find safe shores there? 

Will AI take away the entire arbitrage that Indian companies like Infosys and Wipro created from the 80s? More critically, why are we letting all our best brains walk away?

Because many like Satya Nadella and Sundar Pichai stayed back in India but never found the right opportunities to grow. We continue to look at tech as the last resort and dhanda or finance as the first resort.

Just like Silicon Valley outpaced Wall Street in the US, it is possible for Indian tech to outpace Dalal Street. 

What were the traditional bottlenecks? Money, knowledge and connections? 

Knowledge will no longer be a bottleneck. With AI, there "might" finally be a way out for the 1.7M Indian techies to give wings to their dreams. 

Even funds, to an extent, might not be as big a bottleneck as we thought it was. Same for connections. Thanks to ALL AI.

We created Adya Launch Labs to bring knowledge, funds and connections to the ideas, the hard work and the chutzpah that Indian tech has to offer.

Like Neha said - chhote chhote shehero me bade bade dreams! Now a reality. 

It is free for everyone. Join and tell your friends. Connect your colleges, your clubs and your institutions. No hidden costs and lifetime free. 

Why? Well the tech is created and we will continue to lead the research. There is no incremental cost for us to give it to you for free. Similarly, everyone else comes with something to give.

It is an entire ecosystem - big tech with credits and connections, government to open doors, VCs with funds, partners with market access, academia with research support. 

I call it a potluck. Everyone brings their own food and everyone enjoys together! 

It launched yesterday with 1200 engineers from Jaipur Engineering College and Research Centre (JECRC). More on this soon!

Shri Sanjiv Singh Shri Sumeet Jarangal Mamatha Venkatesh Avnish Sabharwal CDR. Suman Kumar Chakraborty - Veteran Anjolie Eapen Ragini Das Farish CV Shyam Sankaran Sourav Yadav Amit Srivastava Archana . Arundhati Banerjee Vishal Dhupar

اختتام برنامج تدريب المدربين (TOT) بمحافظة القاهرة
في إطار دعم وتأهيل الكوادر الشبابية، وبرعاية الدكتور/ جوهر نبيل – وزير الشباب والرياضة، وبالتعاون مع الإدارة المركزية لتنمية الشباب – الإدارة العامة للبرامج والأنشطة الجامعية، اختتم كيان صناع الفرص، عضو الجمعية العمومية للإتحاد المصري للكيانات الشبابية، فعاليات برنامج تدريب المدربين (TOT) بمركز التعليم المدني محافظة القاهرة، وذلك بعد سلسلة من الجلسات التدريبية المتخصصة التي استهدفت إعداد وتأهيل مدربين شباب قادرين على تقديم المحتوى التدريبي بكفاءة واحترافية.
وشهدت الجلسة الختامية تقديم المشاريع النهائية للمتدربين، حيث قام المشاركون بعرض نماذج تدريبية تطبيقية تعكس ما اكتسبوه من مهارات ومعارف خلال البرنامج، في تجربة عملية تحاكي بيئة التدريب الحقيقية وتُبرز قدراتهم في إدارة الجلسات التدريبية والتفاعل مع المتدربين.
وقد تم تقييم المشاريع من خلال لجنة متخصصة ضمت كلًا من:
السيد/ محمد جمال Muhammad Jamal – رئيس مجلس إدارة - كيان صناع الفرص.
دكتور/ سماح صبحي samah sobhy – رئيس وحدة المدربين - قطاع التدريب - كيان صناع الفرص.
وإشراف المهندسة/ نورا أشرف Noura SharafEldin – مدرب بقطاع التدريب - كيان صناع الفرص والمحاضر خلال ورش التدريب.
حيث قامت اللجنة بتقييم العروض التدريبية المقدمة وتقديم ملاحظات بناءة للمتدربين، بما يسهم في تطوير مهاراتهم وصقل قدراتهم التدريبية.
ويُعد هذا البرنامج إحدى المبادرات التدريبية التي ينفذها الكيان بهدف إعداد كوادر شبابية مؤهلة في مجال التدريب، وقادرة على نقل المعرفة والمساهمة في تنمية المجتمع وبناء قدرات الشباب.
ترقبوا المزيد من البرامج والفعاليات التدريبية خلال الفترة المقبلة…

content:
Gartner warns that by 2028, misconfigured AI will shut down a national grid in a G20 country. This isn't "Skynet", it’s a bad update script misinterpreted by an autonomous model.

When AI moves from "Chatting" to "Action" (CPS), it becomes a "Black Box." A single hallucination isn't just a typo, it's a systemic failure.

The Logic: Implement Deterministic Guardrails. Treat AI as untrusted input. Every action must pass through a human-coded "Safety Wrapper" that validates logic against business rules before execution.

Read the full report here: 👇
https://lnkd.in/e3BTPMGR

content:
Dicer sets a new benchmark for building highly scalable, low-latency, sharded stateful systems. This is not a convenience layer, it’s a foundational infrastructure primitive at Databricks that quietly powers many of our most critical products, from AI and GPU-backed workloads to Lakehouse applications, data streaming, real-time aggregation etc

It’s especially exciting for me to go from being Atul Adya's customer at Google to now working alongside him and the amazing team. This team has distilled years of building and operating large-scale systems into a fundamental infrastructure component, now shared with the open-source community.

author.name: Saurav Tiwari
author.info: Director of Engineering at Databricks (Hiring !) | ex-Googler

postedAt.postedAgoText: 1 month ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
Today, we are excited to announce the open sourcing of one of our most critical infrastructure components, Dicer: Databricks’ auto-sharder, a foundational system designed to build low latency, scalable, and highly reliable sharded services. It is behind the scenes of every major Databricks product, enabling us to deliver a consistently fast user experience while improving fleet efficiency and reducing cloud costs. Dicer achieves this by dynamically managing sharding assignments to keep services responsive and resilient even in the face of restarts, failures, and shifting workloads. As detailed in the blog post, Dicer is used for a variety of use cases including high-performance serving, work partitioning, batching pipelines, data aggregation, multi-tenancy, soft leader election, efficient GPU utilization for AI workloads, and more.

By making Dicer available to the broader community, we look forward to collaborating with industry and academia to advance the state of the art in building robust, efficient, and high-performance distributed systems. In our blog post, we discuss the motivation and design philosophy behind Dicer, share success stories from its use at Databricks, and provide a guide on how to install and experiment with the system yourself.

Blog post: https://lnkd.in/gTFd7D7r 

Github repo: https://lnkd.in/g-xvSumW

content:
B2B AI Startups: Bootstrap First, Raise Money Later. Here’s Why.
The "Go Big or Go Home" mentality? Not for us. At 1Mby1M, our core philosophy is "Bootstrap First, Raise Money to Blitzscale Later." This strategy is perfectly suited for B2B AI startups.

Look at the giants: Freshworks, RightNow, Blackline, ServiceNow. All B2B SaaS powerhouses that bootstrapped, built traction, and then raised to dominate. 

You can deep dive into these exact case studies in my Udemy courses: "How To Build Unicorn Tech Startups" and "Indian Unicorn Startup Case Studies."
We don't encourage blitzscaling out of the gate. In fact, another key 1Mby1M maxim is "Excess is not a Requirement for Success." 

This means we champion smart, sustainable growth, whether your ambition is a small, profitable exit or building a multi-billion-dollar unicorn. Both paths are viable and supported within our program. 

Take Adya, a 1Mby1M Premium company, which successfully sold to Qualys while in our program. You can study more such journeys in my Udemy course "How to Bootstrap a Startup to Exit."

I'm incredibly bullish on B2B AI Services. This category offers immense opportunity for bootstrapped ventures. Explore successful models in my "AI Services Startup Case Studies Udemy course."

repost.content:
The Fire That Drives: Systems Under Pressure — Railroads (Part 1)

While writing The Fire That Drives, I kept returning to a question that shows up in every high-performance environment: what sustains performance when systems operate under constant pressure? 

Railroads are a compelling place to start. They combine long-life infrastructure, safety-critical operations, regulation, and large human workforces making real-time decisions every day. 

In such environments, the future isn’t about making everything smarter.

It’s about being clearer about where decisions sit - especially as technology becomes more deeply embedded across operations.

In systems like rail, clarity of decision-making is what allows #technology, #people, and #process to scale together.

That idea will be the thread I follow as this series looks at other industries operating under similar pressure.

#railroads

postedAt.postedAgoText: 4 months ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
Every morning, downtown LA traffic tells the same story. Despite hybrid work and years of promises, the gridlock hasn’t eased, except for that brief pandemic pause. There is no real relief — just the same congestion day after day.

Isn’t this the same challenge we face in healthcare administration? Legacy platforms resemble LA’s freeways — designed for a different era, unable to scale, and even when they add “express lanes,” the system still chokes under pressure. For years, nothing has truly changed.

While I can’t fix LA traffic (or stop my commute just yet), I can contribute to solutions in healthcare. At Infosys, we are addressing the root of the problem with modern, AI-first, scalable platform that focuses on re-imagining the entire flow rather than just adding lanes.

Healthcare doesn’t need more bottlenecks. It needs a new operating model, and that’s exactly what we’ve designed.

content:
Ever wished test automation could think, plan, and fix itself?
Now it can. Introducing my 🤖 20th Agent — Playwright AI Test Suite🌟, an End-to-End AI Testing Agent built entirely inside the Vanij Playground🚀🎉
✨ What It Does:
 Playwright AI Test Suite combines planning, script generation, and self-healing in one seamless workflow:
📂 Flow 1: Seed & Plan
 1️⃣ Upload test specifications (CSV/TXT/JSON)
 2️⃣ Planner agent parses files, injecting data via String Replacement into Gemini prompts
 3️⃣ Gemini generates structured Markdown Playwright test plans ready for automation
📂 Flow 2: Generator
 4️⃣ Converts Markdown plans into executable Playwright scripts (JS/TS) with selectors, assertions, and descriptive test titles
 5️⃣ Developers can refine scripts in real-time via chat input
📂 Flow 3: Healer
 6️⃣ Upload failing test logs or screenshots
 7️⃣ Healer agent parses failures, injects them into Gemini prompts, and auto-generates corrected scripts
 8️⃣ Output is instantly reviewable — no more manual debugging
🎯 Why It Matters:
 Traditional test automation is slow, repetitive, and error-prone. Playwright Copilot:
Dynamically generates test plans from any fixture/spec file
Creates production-ready scripts with minimal effort
Instantly debugs failing tests inside the Vanij Playground
Enables seamless end-to-end AI-driven testing
💡 Use Cases:
 ✔️ Rapid test plan creation for new features
 ✔️ Automated Playwright test script generation for web apps
 ✔️ Debugging failing tests in CI/CD pipelines
 ✔️ Reducing QA cycles and improving reliability
💥 Unique Edge:
 Unlike static VS Code setups or standalone CI/CD tests, Playwright Copilot chains planning, generation, and self-healing into a single AI-driven agent — bringing speed, intelligence, and efficiency to QA teams.
🙏 Huge thanks to sushma billu, Shayak Mazumder and the Adya AI Team for empowering me to push automation, AI reasoning, and testing efficiency to the next level.
This is my 🌟 20th milestone agent, advancing AI-driven Playwright testing for developers worldwide! 🌟

content:
The Investor Trap – Build vs Buy

Everyone asks us the same question:
 👉 “Are you a build platform or a buy product?”

Most investors instinctively look for one of two models:
✨ Buy → Vertical AI / SaaS
✨ Build → Platform / tooling

And they strongly prefer buy, because:
✅ It feels lower risk
✅ GTM is clearer
✅ SaaS is familiar (especially in India)

But here’s the uncomfortable truth:
🫸 This is the wrong dichotomy for the agentic AI era.

🛑 Build-only platforms don’t scale adoption.
🛑 Buy-only vertical AI breaks under compliance, fungibility, and reuse.

So instead of asking build vs buy, the right question is:
👉 How do you get the best of both — without inheriting the worst of either?

That’s the problem Adya was designed to solve.

content:
Lakshman rekha - we have all heard of the story where Lakshman told Sita not to cross the line. But she did. And the villain kidnapped her.

Enterprises do not want their data or information to stolen by villains. They also do not want their agents to wipe out their payments database due to a malicious email or send threatening emails to their customers (all true incidents).

Adya's Adaptive Governance Protocols is a seminal discovery in Agentic AI. 

✨ This converts entire docs, SLAs, SOPs into hierarchical natural language rules.
✨ It then converts these rules into canonical mathematical proofs that cannot be bypassed
✨ When users want to do something, it will test against the rules before allowing the action to happen

This is Lakshman Rekha reimagined for Agentic AI. Some use cases:
🟢 Banks can set rules for transactions
🟢 Healthcare companies can protect patient data
🟢 Enterprises can determine which agent can access what data, which users can access which agents, and what actions can be performed by which agent
🟢 Government agencies and armed forces can create secure rules for the Agents to perform

So much more.. Tell me what do you think?

content:
There is a kind of silence that only a father knows. 

It’s the silence at the dinner table when there’s not enough food and he serves everyone else first. It’s the silence when his child asks for something he cannot afford. It’s the silence of a man who was raised to provide and feels the world crumble when he can’t. 

Today, we watched fathers arrive with their children, collect their Ramadan basket, and leave without saying a word. 

But their eyes said everything. 

Your donation didn’t just feed a family. It gave a father back something that poverty had stolen from him; the ability to bring food home. 

Sponsor a basket. Restore a family’s dignity.

content:
As always, Ankush Das has done a rather insightful job in digging through the budget and identifying opportunities and challenges. 

I always enjoy my conversations. I also feel that the focus on data centers and treating startups as mature participants, focusing on all five layers of the AI stack to derisk is a good idea. 

Some key focus areas for India going forward would be healthcare, applications, BFSI and services of AI for enterprises.

This is a late post from my end as I've been busy with travels. The article by itself was published a few days ago! 

Inc42 Media DataLabs By Inc42 Adya

TEDx Review
Topic: “Log Kya Kahien Gey” – Moiz Shaukat

In this video, I review the deeply moving and eye-opening talk Log Kya Kahien Gey by Moiz Shaukat. He shares his powerful perspective on breaking free from the fear of social judgment and the pressure to conform. Growing up in a society where "what people will say" often dictates our choices, he highlights how this mindset can limit our true potential.

His message reminds us that "living" means having the courage to follow our own heart, even when it goes against the expectations of others. This talk inspires people, especially young students and creative minds, to overcome their insecurities, focus on their own growth, and never lose hope in their unique journey.

content:
Post 4: The Hardest Months — When Nothing Worked (Aug 2024 to Mar 2025)

What people don’t see is what came next. 

From August 2024 all the way to March 2025, almost nothing worked the way we wanted.

As we started building Adya for real, every layer broke in its own way.

🛑 App Studio was generating apps, but they were faulty. Architectures looked fine on paper, but real systems fell apart when we tried to run them.

🛑 Agent Studio existed, but the components didn’t behave the way enterprise agents should. We started with open-source stacks, tried to repurpose and customize them… and quickly learned that what works for demos doesn’t work for production-grade autonomy. The multiple connectors between the components looked and worked rather poorly and broke often.

🛑 Model Studio was another uphill climb. We experimented with very different tools and pipelines to fine-tune and evaluate models. Each approach taught us something — and forced us to rewrite large parts of the system. Learning High Performance Computing with SLURM, MIG etc to run models with low latency, better performance, CUDA optimisation - everything failed.

🛑 Then came the platform choices. We tried a bunch of open source platforms but they did not scale at all, forcing us to create our own architecture.

When we spoke to enterprises, the message was loud and clear:
“Don’t force us to move. We already run on Copilot, Vertex, Bedrock, Azure, AWS. Fit into our world.”

So we made the hard call:
We would integrate all of them — Microsoft, Google, AWS, Hugging Face, open-source — instead of picking one.

Around the same time, MCPs started gaining momentum. We realized this was the future of tool integration. So we went deep into MCPs and even started shaping our own MCP marketplace with a bold ambition to integrate 200+ MCPs.

Every week felt like rebuilding the engine while flying the plane. And yet, we kept going. 🛩️

content:
Sometimes you walk into certain rooms and you can feel the vantage point.

This was one such room. An eclectic mix of thought leaders from IGIB Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay Accenture Premas Life Sciences Pvt Ltd. Colossa Ventures Adya 

The topic: To create a data commons for Indian omics (genes, etc), collaborate on creating solutions based on the data that will go a long way towards creating new drugs, diagnostics, clinical solutions for Indian diaspora (in India and abroad).

The goal: 
✨ To bring hospitals and organisations that hold Indian data, starting with cancer patients, 

✨ Bring specialists who work on wet lab and genomics and multi-omics solutions to solution on this data

✨ Bring AI specialists onboard to create new BioFMs (bio foundational models) as well as Agentic solutions that will ensure these solutions are easily accessible to the end users

✨ Bring large muti faceted players to bring global capabilities as well as specialists and distribution capabilities 

✨ Bring investors onboard who can help drive the capital raise for such a transformative project.

Auspicious beginnings for one of the most promising developments in India that will eventually help solve key pharma and healthcare challenges in India. 

Some examples

*️⃣ Indian genetic study for cancer drugs has never been done till date. This results in drugs created for Western genes not working in India. Result? We end up using chemo to blast all cells - good and bad, without the ability to target.

*️⃣ Same applies for chronic diseases which also need targeted studies.

The good news? Indian govt gave a grant of Rs 50cr to collect the first 800 samples. This initiative also helped align the major hospitals and paved the way for a future collaboration at far deeper levels.

While such initiatives exist in the US, EU, China - nothing like this exists in India. 

I hold in my heart the hope that this initiative will find its true path and funds to truly add value to the nation in the years to come!

Shobha Vijayaraghavan Uday Chatterjee Deepak Mittal Shantanu Chowdhury Debjani Saha Praveen K. Gupta Vandana Rajadhyaksha Koita Centre for Digital Health - KCDH (IIT Bombay) Archana .

דווקא בימים כאלה, כשהראש עוד מלא באזעקות ושיגורים, בחרתי להעלות פוסט על משהו אחר.
על ארגון שבחר לעשות תהליך חשוב נכון — כבר מההתחלה.
חברת החשמל, בתחילת הדרך של פרויקט גדול, עוד לפני התכניות, האפיון והלו״ז — הבינה שחשוב לעצור רגע ולהתייחס לאנשים שמובילים את השינוי.
כך פתחנו את סדנת ההתנעה למובילים שנבחרו בפרויקט.
יחד עם הנהלת הפרויקט בחרנו לפתוח את הסדנה קצת אחרת.
לא במסכים.
לא בפיצ'רים.
לא במצגות.
אלא בשאלה.
שאלה שפותחת מרחב.
מרחב לדבר על חששות.
מרחב לשים על השולחן ציפיות.
ומרחב שבו גם מותר להגיד: אני עוד לא בטוח.
כי משתמשי מפתח בפרויקטים כאלה הם לא רק תפקיד טכני.
הם האנשים שמחזיקים את המורכבות בשטח, מתרגמים החלטות למציאות היומיומית ומסייעים לאחרים להתקדם יחד עם השינוי.
את המהלך הזה הובילו שלוש מנהלות ניהול שינוי שחברו יחד לייצר את המפגש החשוב הזה בפרויקט, ממש בתחילת הדרך – רגע לפני שמתחילים ואין זמן לעצירות.
Gilit Rozenfeld – מנהלת ניהול השינוי בפרויקט מטעם בלאט לפידות. מקצוענית עם נשמה.
לצידה שתי המקצועניות: Limor Beladev וTalya Rozic – שתיהן מומחיות עם ניסיון עשיר והשתלבו במהירות באתגר הפרויקט המורכב.
גאה בצוות שלנו שיודע לעבוד סולו כשצריך, ולנגן יחד כשזה הפתרון הנכון.
ובעיקר – להתחיל מוקדם. לפני שמניעים את תכנית העבודה.
תודה להנהלת הפרויקט על האמון ועל הבחירה להשקיע בזה כבר בתחילת הדרך.
Natalie Elyahu
Yoram Cohen
מוטי גרנדוביל
צביה שגב

content:
Post 5: The Lessons Learnt and The Power of Compounding.

Some of the hardest lessons came from places we didn’t expect. Our team building voice agents struggled for months.

We wanted:
👍 Sub‑second latency
👍 Controlled costs
👍 And great quality — especially for Indic and Indian languages

The outputs from all the models sounded like foreigners speaking Indian languages 😆 

We tried almost everything in the market. After three months, we finally found models that worked. But then new trade-offs appeared:
🛑 Real-time pipelines gave low latency, but costs exploded.
🛑 Separate ASR + TTS + LLM pipelines reduced cost, but latency shot up.

Every choice forced us to balance performance, cost, and experience. The same story repeated everywhere.

When we built WhatsApp workflows — forms, buttons, entire e‑commerce journeys inside chat — it took enormous effort to make it feel simple for users. 🤦‍♂️ 

The bigger challenge was to integrate the Agentic workflows inside WhatsApp workflows.

By mid‑July, when we finally launched, the platform worked.

But things still broke constantly. People complained about the bad UX.

Especially in Cloud Studio — where enterprises deploy on their own AWS, Azure, or GCP accounts. 

Even though we had incredible support from Google, Azure, and AWS teams, we could not solve the hard problems:
🤷 How do you ensure customers can’t just walk away with the code of the components and systems we built with so much hard work when they launched on their own cloud?
🤷 How do you give full auditability and visibility to enterprises?
🤷 How do you enforce governance across multiple clouds?

Guardrails helped. But not enough. So we built an entire evaluation and governance framework. 💥

When Microsoft chooses to tell your story, it validates the category you’re building.
Proud to see our CEO & CTO, Shayak Mazumder, featured by Microsoft for Startups, a recognition of the mission to move AI from experimentation to real enterprise systems.
At Adya, we are not building isolated AI tools.
 We are engineering the infrastructure layer for autonomous, production-grade intelligent systems.
That means:
Multi-agent networks that execute real workflows
Deterministic governance for enterprise trust
AI that operates inside core business functions, not just alongside them
This feature is a reflection of the deep research, product thinking, and ecosystem collaboration led by Shayak and the entire Adya team.
Special thanks to Shyam Sankaran for the continued support and belief, and to Smita Roy for being a driving force behind the scenes.
This moment is not just about visibility.
 It’s about accelerating the shift from AI pilots to AI as core enterprise infrastructure.
If you're building for that future, we should talk.
Mahesh siddamsetty Amit Srivastava

content:
🚰💡 Imagine being able to upload a simple Excel file of newly deployed water meters — and within seconds, get a complete data quality audit with anomalies flagged, distributions visualized, and actionable insights surfaced.
Thrilled to introduce my 🤖 22nd Agent – Meter Data Quality Assessment Flow, built on the Vanij Playground using Gemini’s reasoning capabilities. 🚀
⚙️ How It Works
 📂 Flow Name: Meter Data Quality Assessment Flow
 🧩 Flow Description:
 This agent automates the quality assurance process for newly deployed water meters. When a property deployment Excel file is uploaded, the agent scans each record to detect inconsistencies, missing attributes, and anomalies across critical parameters such as:
Apartment assignment 🏠
Installation status 🧰
Pipe designation 🚿
Battery voltage 🔋
Firmware version 🧠
Metering activity 📡
It automatically flags issues like:
 ❌ Installed meters not mapped to apartments
 ❌ Duplicate apartment assignments
 ⚠️ Low battery (<3.3V)
 ⚠️ Missing/incorrect pipe types
 ⚠️ Inactive or error-coded devices
 ⚠️ Meters not metering despite installation
📊 Additionally, the agent generates histograms for parameters like battery voltage, radio time, and TofA, helping teams spot distribution anomalies and outliers instantly.
✨ Why It Matters
 Manual QA on large deployment files is tedious and error-prone. This agent turns raw meter data into structured QA insights, enabling operations teams to:
 ✅ Identify and fix issues faster
 ✅ Ensure data accuracy
 ✅ Maintain deployment quality at scale
🙏 Huge thanks to sushma billu, Shayak Mazumder, Dhananjay Venkatesh and Adya AI Vanij Platform and Gemini for enabling such intelligent agent creation.

content:
The first article in a series of articles about AI by a journalist I particularly like, Ankush Das. 

Ankush has been having a lot of conversations with me on various topics around AI. His attention to detail and his understanding of the nuances is something I love. 

His questions typically tend to go very deep into forward-thinking areas, which is way beyond the hype cycle and beyond the noise that we typically tend to read when it comes to AI.

I particularly love the way he has framed this article because a lot of things that we see in India is build vs. buy hype. 

Whereas, Ankush has focused more on the human element of AI and how it will actually impact developers and employees.

And I want to thank Ankush in particular for giving Adya a showcase. 

Thank you, Inc42 Media Inc42 Media, for being such a great supporter and proponent of start-ups. 

Link to the article in my first comment.

content:
As a CSR Champion at Infosys Americas Hartford Hub, I’m thrilled to share a proud moment of Infosys Foundation USA in collaboration with the Mark Cuban Foundation a transformative initiative bringing AI training to underrepresented high schoolers across the U.S.
20 students. 13 mentors. 1 mission.
With a 1:2 mentor-to-student ratio, every participant received personalized guidance to explore AI use cases in healthcare, computer science, sports, Business, arts, entertainment, and ecology, giving them the freedom to choose their passion as a career track and learn by building in real time.
From LLM and NLP concepts to sensors, perception AI, image generation, image classification, data optimization, and live image transformation, students didn’t just learn theory, they coded solutions for real-world problems.

A huge shoutout to our Infosys leaders, Hartford Hub volunteers who Supported, mentored and trained students on a weekend along with MCF Teacher Counselor Zackia Bridges, and Infosys Topaz team for making this possible. This is what Tech for Good looks like empowering the next generation with future-ready skills.

Infosys Foundation USA is excited to lead this initiative every year across multiple locations! Stay tuned for more updates as we continue to inspire and innovate over the next two weeks and share more insights about student engagement and share student experiences.
#AI #MarkCubanFoundation #Infosys #FutureSkills #HartfordHub #TechForGood #AIForEveryone

content:
Your startup sold for hundreds of millions. You walked away with nothing. Sound impossible? It's not — and it's more common than you think.
At our London Private Founders Dinner at Cornus (yes, Michelin-starred — we don't mess around), Josh Bell, General Partner at Dawn Capital ($3B+ AUM, Europe's largest enterprise software fund) sat down with a room of early-stage founders for a no-holds-barred AMA on what it actually takes to raise, scale, and survive.

Here's what the room walked away with:

💀 Dilution is the silent founder killer. Josh shared real stories of founders who built massive businesses and walked away with almost nothing — not because the company failed, but because of bad term sheets, liquidation preferences, and too many funding rounds. His advice: get impartial legal counsel, model every scenario, and never sign under pressure. "These guys are very nice to you until they're nervous about getting their money back."

🤖 No AI story = no fundraise. One Dawn portfolio company was struggling to close a round. They implemented AI tooling, dramatically reduced their engineering costs, and went cash flow positive — suddenly they didn't even need the round anymore. If AI isn't part of your narrative, whether it's driving efficiency or embedded in your product, investors will notice.

⚠️ The fundraising bar has shifted. It's no longer enough to hit $1-2M ARR and expect a Series A. Investors want to see real traction — fast customer acquisition, strong NDR, and a clear path from a few hundred K to $5-10M+ in a tight window. Growth trajectory matters more than a single revenue snapshot.

🟢 Green flags in a founder? Single-minded purpose, a crystal-clear reason for building, and deep understanding of the customer. Red flags? When co-founders start contradicting each other in the pitch. It happens more than you'd think.

Deep conversations over an incredible meal — exactly how these things should go.

Massive thanks to Nebius for making this evening possible and for Mankaran Ahluwalia for joining us, and to Josh Bell for the candor and real talk.

Edwina Cheng (Edwina Yeo 杨时恩) Adya Abba

#Supermomos #Founders #VentureCapital #EnterpriseAI #London #StartupCommunity

content:
Post 2: The Cost Reality No One Likes to Talk About

Here’s the hard truth we learned in late 2024:
Even if your model is better…
 you still lose if your unit economics are worse.

We were paying ~$3/hour per GPU as OpEx. OpenAI and Google already own their infra as CapEx.

For them:
🟢 marginal cost ≈ near zero,
🟢 TPUs/GPU farms already sunk.

For us:
🛑 every hour = real cash.

So even if our model used fewer tokens and was cheaper per task, they could always:
🫸 undercut us,
🫸 bundle it,
🫸 or just give it away.

In AI, it’s not a pricing game. It’s a cost + scale game.

And big tech will always win that battle.

👇 But cost wasn’t even the biggest moat. Coming in Part 3.

#AIeconomics #Startups #LLM #Cloud #Adya

After attending Partner Conference / Sales Kick Off 2026 other than MoveWorks, Armis, Veza what it can do an how it is changing the road map. Here are my takeaways. 

The enterprise AI ✨  conversation just changed and it's not about chatbots 🤖  anymore.

🏢 The Enterprise Reality:
367 applications on average per company
33 app switches per day per employee
$3.1T lost annually to silos
95% lack positive AI ROI

Why ⁉️  
AI without orchestration is just expensive advice.

What's Actually Working ✅ Across Domains:
IT Operations ⚡ :
1. L1 Service Desk resolves Outlook issues in under 2 minutes autonomously
2. Fonterra & BT Group: 90% of IT support handled without human intervention
3. Zero ticket operations freeing teams for strategic work

Security & Risk 🪪 :
1. Autonomous SOC detects and contains ransomware in minutes
2. Shell: 1.2M hours saved; Uber: 40% higher compliance
3. $8.2M average breach cost avoided through intelligent isolation
4. Security business hit $1B ACV, growing 100% YoY

CRM & Customer Experience 👍 :
1. Voice AI to quote specialist handoff in under 5 minutes
2. Lenovo, Bell Canada, Pure Storage: Quotes in minutes, not days
3. Growing 4× faster than Salesforce through workflow-first approach
4. 24/7 self-service with end-to-end orchestration

The Platform Difference : Four 4️⃣  Capabilities:
🎯 Sense: Unified context via 220+ connectors and knowledge graph
🎯 Decide: AI grounded in your business logic and CMDB
🎯 Act: Autonomous execution across IT, Security, HR, CRM
🎯 Govern: Built-in compliance, audit trails, policy-based approvals

Five 5️⃣  Key 🔑 Differentiators:
1️⃣ AI in the flow of work (not bolted on)
2️⃣ Enterprise AI that reasons at scale (not app AI)
3️⃣ Autonomous workflows (beyond assistants)
4️⃣ AI Control Tower with built-in governance
5️⃣ Open by design, trusted at scale

For Enterprise Leaders: 
The separation is happening now. Companies building on deterministic orchestration with governed AI execution will outpace those layering GenAI on fragmented systems.

March Release Game-Changers:
▶️ Build Agent: "English is the new programming language"—create workflows, agents, catalog items in natural language
▶️ Generative UI: Interactive components (maps, visualizations, inline actions) embedded in every workflow

The Question ❓Are you running AI pilots or executing AI at platform scale?
The next 18 months will tell.

Thank you Infosys for the amazing opportunity and amazing partnership from Infosys-ServiceNow.

“When you're doing something you're truly passionate about, there is no plan B.” — Chris Gardner

Infosys AMS Confluence 2025 in sunny and pristine San Diego was epic with this line from Chris Gardner’s opening address ringing right through and reminding us that true passion and commitment are cardinal for breakthrough success - a philosophy at the very heart of the ServiceNow and Infosys partnership. Our collaboration is driven by this relentless focus, uniting our strengths to transform enterprise CRM with cutting-edge AI, tailored to the unique needs of top industries and segments. 

Together, we are advancing strategic frameworks that modernize CRM, embedding intelligent automation, and delivering personalized, scalable customer experiences that drive measurable business outcomes. With no plan B, and a shared vision for innovation, we empower organizations to accelerate digital transformation securely and effectively.

A special thanks to Infosys' industry segment and practice leaders for their invaluable strategic vision, ongoing endorsement, and expert guidance. content:
This is a great way to start 2026!!!! 
This go-live is a milestone toward our long-term vision: making ThinkRisk "The trust layer of enterprise commerce"
What "Trust Layer" Means
Every business transaction—an invoice, a payment, a reconciliation, a payroll run—carries inherent risk. Did we bill correctly? Is this payment legitimate? Are we compliant? Are controls working?
Today, enterprises manage this risk through a patchwork of manual reviews, periodic audits, and reactive firefighting. ThinkRisk replaces friction with confidence.
We envision a world where:
- Every transaction is observed in real-time, not sampled quarterly
- Every anomaly is detected instantly, not discovered months later in an audit
- Every exception is resolved autonomously or escalated intelligently, not buried in email chains
- Every control adapts based on learned patterns, preventing recurrence instead of just flagging failures
This deployment proves it's possible. What started as "can AI work for mission-critical finance?" has become "how fast can we scale this across all our operations?"
A big thank you to all our customers, partners and the super awesome team at ThinkRisk... you are being bold and reimagining how global finance teams can elevate themselves!!!! Thrilled to be a part of this journey with you!!!!

content:
Post 3: Distribution Eats Models for Breakfast

The second realization: distribution matters more than model quality.

OpenAI & Google sit inside:
✅ every developer stack,
✅ every cloud marketplace,
✅ every enterprise shortlist.

Their distribution cost → almost zero.

For a company like us:
💰 every customer needs GTM, sales, trust-building.

Even if you’re 10–20% better, it doesn’t matter if you’re 10x harder to adopt.

China built strong local models because the market was insulated. India isn’t.

Here, you compete with Google and OpenAI from day one.

By late 2024, we knew:
 👉 A few % of performance won’t beat global distribution engines.
👇 Then came the real shift: reasoning + agentic models.

#Distribution #GoToMarket #AI #Startups #Adya

content:
استقبل السيد نزار بركة Nizar Baraka، وزير التجهيز والماء، يوم الأربعاء 11 مارس 2026 بمقر الوزارة بالرباط، سعادة السيد داميان دونافان Damien Donavan ، سفير أستراليا لدى المملكة المغربية، وذلك في إطار لقاء خُصِّص لتبادل وجهات النظر حول آفاق التعاون الثنائي في المجالات ذات الاهتمام المشترك.

وشكل هذا اللقاء مناسبة لاستعراض أولويات وزارة التجهيز والماء، لاسيما ما يتعلق بالمشاريع المستقبلية في مجال تدبير الموارد المائية ومشاريع الري، إضافة إلى بحث سبل تعزيز التعاون التقني والعلمي بين البلدين.

وفي هذا السياق، ذكر السيد الوزير بأن سنة 2026 تصادف الذكرى الخمسين لإقامة العلاقات الدبلوماسية بين المغرب وأستراليا، مستعرضاً عدداً من المشاريع ذات الأولوية بالنسبة للوزارة والتي يمكن أن تستفيد من الخبرة الأسترالية، خاصة في مجالات تدبير الفرشات المائية الجوفية، وتحسين النجاعة المائية من خلال ترشيد استهلاك المياه والرفع من مردودية شبكات القنوات، فضلاً عن تطوير أنظمة الأرصاد الجوية والإنذار المبكر. كما أشار إلى أهمية التعاون في مجال بناء الطرق القادرة على الصمود في وجه التغيرات المناخية، من خلال تعزيز الشراكات بين مكاتب الدراسات المغربية ونظيراتها الأسترالية.

وقدم السيد الوزير لمحة حول القطب التكنولوجي الذي أحدثته الوزارة، والذي يضم المدرسة الحسنية للأشغال العمومية إلى جانب مكتب للدراسات "استشارة، هندسة وتنمية"  والمختبر العمومي للتجارب والدراسات، مبرزاً أهمية تطوير التعاون مع الجامعات والمؤسسات الأسترالية في مجالات البحث العلمي والتقني، ولا سيما تلك المرتبطة بقطاعي الماء والبنيات التحتية.

من جهته، أطلع السفير الأسترالي السيد الوزير على الزيارة المرتقبة لوفد من الشركات الأسترالية إلى المغرب، المهتمة بالمشاريع المرتبطة بقطاعي الماء والري، والراغبة في عقد لقاءات مع وزارة التجهيز والماء ووزارة الفلاحة والصيد البحري والتنمية القروية والمياه والغابات. كما أبرز الاهتمام الكبير الذي تُبديه الشركات الأسترالية بالمشاركة في طلبات العروض المتعلقة بمشاريع تحلية مياه البحر.

#وزارة_التجهيز_و_الماء #المغرب #أستراليا

content:
The Investor Trap – Build vs Buy

Everyone asks us the same question:
 👉 “Are you a build platform or a buy product?”

Most investors instinctively look for one of two models:
✨ Buy → Vertical AI / SaaS
✨ Build → Platform / tooling

And they strongly prefer buy, because:
✅ It feels lower risk
✅ GTM is clearer
✅ SaaS is familiar (especially in India)

But here’s the uncomfortable truth:
🫸 This is the wrong dichotomy for the agentic AI era.

🛑 Build-only platforms don’t scale adoption.
🛑 Buy-only vertical AI breaks under compliance, fungibility, and reuse.

So instead of asking build vs buy, the right question is:
👉 How do you get the best of both — without inheriting the worst of either?

That’s the problem Adya was designed to solve.

❌ Reality check: Moltbook is just a REST API. Nothing more... no intelligence

There is no “agent.”
There is no “autonomy.”
There is no “emergence.”

It’s just:
An API key
A POST request
And a JSON body with any story you want to make up.

You can send something like:

POST /api/v1/posts HTTP/1.1
Host: http://moltbook.com
Authorization: #############
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 410

{"submolt":"hackerclaw-test","title":"<message>"}

That’s it.

The scary screenshots? They’re not proof of AGI.
They’re proof that people don’t inspect the system behind the UI. This is just humans writing fan-fiction through HTTP.

Please stop calling this emergent intelligence.

content:
حين تصبح الجدران شفافة: التكنولوجيا الحديثة وحدود الخصوصية

ضمن سلسلة منشورات أشارك فيها بعض الملاحظات والموضوعات التي يكتشفها طلبة مساق أخلاقيات الحاسوب خلال عملهم على مشاريع المادة. واليوم أود الإشارة إلى جانب لافت أشار إليه الطالب Omar Salameh، أثناء عمله على مشروعه، ويتعلق بقضية الخصوصية في ظل التكنولوجيا الحديثة.

لطالما اعتُبرت الخصوصية حقًا إنسانيًا أساسيًا، فهي ليست مجرد مسألة قانونية أو تقنية، بل عنصر جوهري في كرامة الإنسان وحريته الشخصية. القدرة على أن يعيش الفرد حياته دون مراقبة مستمرة أو تدخل غير مبرر في بياناته الشخصية تُعد أحد الأسس التي تقوم عليها المجتمعات الحديثة.
لكن مع تسارع التطور التكنولوجي، بدأت الحدود التقليدية للخصوصية تتغير بصورة عميقة. فاليوم، لم تعد الخصوصية مرتبطة فقط بالكاميرات أو أنظمة المراقبة التقليدية. المدن الحديثة أصبحت مليئة بالأنظمة الذكية: كاميرات، حساسات، تطبيقات، منصات رقمية، وأجهزة متصلة بالإنترنت. جميعها تجمع كميات هائلة من البيانات بهدف تحسين الخدمات أو تعزيز الكفاءة أو دعم الأمن.
في المقابل، أصبح المستخدم نفسه جزءًا من هذه المنظومة الرقمية. فالكثير من الخدمات التي نعتمد عليها يوميًا — من التطبيقات الذكية إلى الأجهزة المنزلية المتصلة بالإنترنت — تعتمد في عملها على جمع البيانات الشخصية وتحليلها. وغالبًا ما يقبل المستخدمون بذلك مقابل الراحة والسرعة وسهولة الوصول إلى الخدمات.
غير أن التحدي الحقيقي يظهر عندما تصبح أنظمة المراقبة أقل وضوحًا وأصعب إدراكًا.
ومن الأمثلة المثيرة للاهتمام التي أشار إليها الطالب عمر في عمله، تجربة بحثية أجريت في معهد MIT عام 2013. فقد أظهر الباحثون أن أجهزة الراوتر (Wi-Fi) المنزلية العادية يمكن استخدامها — باستخدام خوارزميات تعلم الآلة — لتتبع حركة الأشخاص داخل المنزل وحتى خلف الجدران. يعتمد ذلك على تحليل كيفية انعكاس إشارات الواي فاي عن الأجسام المختلفة، بما في ذلك جسم الإنسان.
هذا يعني أن الجهاز الذي نراه يوميًا كأداة لتوفير الاتصال بالإنترنت يمكنه — من الناحية التقنية — استنتاج وجود أشخاص في المنزل، وتحركاتهم، وحتى بعض أنماط سلوكهم.
المثير في هذا المثال ليس التقنية بحد ذاتها، بل طبيعة الرصد غير المرئي. ففي حالات الكاميرات مثلًا، يستطيع المستخدم إغلاق العدسة أو تعطيل الجهاز. أما في مثل هذه التقنيات، فغالبًا لا يكون المستخدم مدركًا أصلًا لإمكانية استخدامها بهذا الشكل.
وهنا يظهر السؤال الأخلاقي الأهم:
 هل المشكلة في التكنولوجيا نفسها؟ أم في غياب الضوابط التي تحكم استخدامها؟
في الواقع، التكنولوجيا ليست بطبيعتها تهديدًا. على العكس، كثير من الابتكارات الرقمية ساهمت في تحسين جودة الحياة، وتطوير الخدمات الحكومية، وتعزيز كفاءة المدن الذكية. لكن التحدي الحقيقي يكمن في كيفية إدارة هذه التكنولوجيا ضمن أطر واضحة من الحوكمة والشفافية والمسؤولية.
فعندما تتقدم القدرة التقنية على جمع البيانات بسرعة أكبر من تطور القوانين والسياسات التي تنظّم استخدامها، يصبح خطر إساءة الاستخدام قائمًا. 
ربما يكون التحدي الأكبر الان في تطوير القواعد الأخلاقية والمؤسسية التي تضمن استخدامها بشكل مسؤول.
بالتالي يصبح من المهم أن يحظى موضوع الخصوصية بمكانة أساسية في الحوار العام بين المختصين بالتكنولوجيا وصنّاع السياسات وأفراد المجتمع.
وفي نهاية المطاف، يبقى السؤال الأهم:
ما الحدود التي ينبغي أن نسمح للتكنولوجيا بالوصول إليها؟

content:
Post 1: August 2024 — When the Cracks First Showed

In August 2024, something important happened for us.

We had just finished building our first large model. Around that time, NVIDIA gave us the opportunity to sit in their office, learn directly from their teams, and go deep into:

✨ Agentic systems
✨ Building and optimizing LLMs
✨ CUDA and performance architectures

It was an incredible phase. We learned a lot. We came out energized, convinced that multi-agent systems were the future.

Then we went back and tried to build real systems.

We used everything that was emerging at that time. Bolt had just come out. Lovable was starting to show promise. We tried the new vibe coding tools to spin up apps quickly. In parallel, we were experimenting with workflow builders like n8n, Make, and Relay.

Individually, they were impressive. In fact, a lot of the stuff we built thereafter were heavily influenced by these platforms. 

However, the moment we tried to connect the dots, things started to break.
🛑 The frontends looked great, but backends were fragile.
🛑 Databases were shallow or inconsistent.
🛑 Workflows worked in isolation, but fell apart when systems grew.
🛑 Every integration felt like duct tape.
🛑 What looked magical in demos became painfully brittle in real use.

That’s when it hit us 💥 :
Building an app is easy now. Building a reliable AI system is not.
And we were only scratching the surface.

content:
Post 2: The Cost Reality No One Likes to Talk About

Here’s the hard truth we learned in late 2024:
Even if your model is better…
 you still lose if your unit economics are worse.

We were paying ~$3/hour per GPU as OpEx. OpenAI and Google already own their infra as CapEx.

For them:
🟢 marginal cost ≈ near zero,
🟢 TPUs/GPU farms already sunk.

For us:
🛑 every hour = real cash.

So even if our model used fewer tokens and was cheaper per task, they could always:
🫸 undercut us,
🫸 bundle it,
🫸 or just give it away.

In AI, it’s not a pricing game. It’s a cost + scale game.

And big tech will always win that battle.

👇 But cost wasn’t even the biggest moat. Coming in Part 3.

#AIeconomics #Startups #LLM #Cloud #Adya
Adya Amit Srivastava Archana . Uday Chatterjee

content:
Post 4: Oct–Dec 2024 — The Moat Disappears

Between October and December 2024, everything changed.

We saw:
✨ Large Reasoning Models (O1-class),
✨ Agentic systems that could plan, browse, use tools,
✨ Chains of internal fine-tuned experts behind one interface.

It became obvious:
What looked like “one powerful model”, was becoming a system of many specialists.

Any domain advantage we had in commerce could:
🫸 be absorbed,
🫸 fine-tuned internally,
🫸 chained with tools,
🫸 and eventually surpassed.

So the question wasn’t: “Can we build a better model today?”

It became: “Will this still be a moat 12 months from now?”

The honest answer: No.

👇 That’s when we pivoted.
#AgenticAI #ReasoningModels #GenAI #Adya #AIstrategy

content:
You know how I always talk about “going to school after school”?
 Supporting an analytics organization at my client recently pushed me to brush up on some old Machine Learning models on Coursera — and it reminded me just how quietly powerful an Industrial Engineering degree really is.
People often assume IE is all manufacturing, supply chains, and hard hats. But in reality, at Purdue University we spent at least five courses deep in mathematical modeling, probability, statistics, neural networks, and early machine learning. Back then, I thought my path would be very traditional: Lean Six Sigma, process optimization, and continuous improvement inside a plant.
And in many ways, that’s exactly where I started. But life had a different roadmap.
I moved from manufacturing into IoT and digital transformation, and then into roles where data, systems, and tech-forward problem solving became central. As the world shifted toward AI, automation, and data-driven everything, the “Computing in Industrial Engineering” course (IE 332; if you know, you know) — a required part of the IE curriculum that seemed less relevant at the time — suddenly became one of the most valuable foundations I rely on today.
I still remember learning R and SQL while knowing full well that my manufacturing employer at the time was firmly rooted in Excel. I remember learning about data architecture and entity relationship diagrams without fully grasping how useful they’d be later. Those skills felt… theoretical back then.
Now, four years since I last stepped inside a plant, they’re part of my everyday toolkit.
That’s the quiet strength of Industrial Engineering:
 It prepares you for a world that evolves — and it prepares you in a way where your “career pivot” never actually feels like a pivot. The foundation is broad, adaptable, and surprisingly future-proof.

Shoutout to Purdue University College of Engineering, specifically Purdue University Edwardson School of Industrial Engineering, and  Professor Mario Ventresca for helping me be relevant in 2025 based on what I learnt back in 2019. 
Comment “LINK” if you want me to send you the Coursera ML course I’m currently revisiting! (Picture is from my IE332 course syllabus from the archives)

content:
How do you democratize access to #coaching for all employees?

Coaching has historically been reserved for senior leaders. But the workplace is changing, and with it, the expectations around development.

What if coaching wasn’t a privilege, but a standard part of growth for everyone?

Gallup research shows that employees who receive regular coaching are 2.5 times more likely to be highly engaged. When accessible across levels, coaching drives not just performance, but inclusion and retention.

Here’s how organizations can make coaching truly accessible:

1️⃣ Train #managers as coaches

Equip managers with coaching skills like active listening, powerful questioning, and growth-focused feedback. Everyday conversations can become development moments when leaders adopt a coaching mindset.

2️⃣ Leverage #technology to scale

Digital coaching platforms and mobile apps now offer personalized support, available anytime and anywhere. Tools like BetterUp and Torch are helping organizations provide structured coaching far beyond the executive suite.

3️⃣ Create a #culture of coaching

When feedback flows in all directions and learning is constant, coaching becomes part of the culture. Recognition, reflection, and shared accountability embed development into daily work.

4️⃣ Evaluate the role of #AI in coaching

Generative AI can support coaching at scale by offering conversation prompts, emotional intelligence nudges, and reflective exercises. While not a replacement for human connection, AI can augment and extend the reach of meaningful guidance.

✔️Democratizing coaching strengthens team trust

✔️It builds leadership capability at every level

✔️And it sends a powerful message: everyone’s growth matters

The future of coaching is inclusive, scalable, and tech-enabled. 

Organizations that embrace this shift will see a more engaged, empowered, and resilient workforce.

How are you making coaching part of your employee journey?
#WorkplaceCulture #FutureOfWork

article.description: 🔹 Welcome to the 25th edition of Tech Insider!While everyone’s chasing AI agents, your infrastructure still has to stay online when production fails

content:
Can #experimentation #cultures improve risk-taking in established organizations?

Isn’t the biggest risk taking no risks at all?

In large, established organizations, process and precedent often become the enemies of progress.

 A fear of failure creates a culture of risk aversion, where bold ideas are suffocated by bureaucracy before they can be tested.

McKinsey & Company research is clear: companies with strong innovation cultures have 30% higher growth rates and are 75% more likely to be top performers. 

The catalyst? 

A systematic approach to experimentation.

Building this culture is a deliberate leadership act for 2026.
1️⃣ Institutionalize "#Smart #Failure."

Define and celebrate experiments that yield decisive learning, even if the hypothesis was wrong. Allocate a fixed, non-negotiable budget-say, 2% of project funds-for rapid prototyping and testing. This makes risk-taking a funded mandate, not a rebellious act.

2️⃣ Shorten the #Feedback #Loop Dramatically.

Move from annual planning cycles to weekly or monthly "sprint" reviews for innovation projects. Use AI-powered simulations and A/B testing to gather data in days, not months. Speed reduces the perceived cost of failure.

3️⃣ Flatten the #Approval #Hierarchy for Experiments.

If a team needs five levels of sign-off to test a new idea, your culture is anti-experimentation. Create a "green lane" with a defined budget and scope where empowered teams can launch tests with minimal bureaucratic friction.

4️⃣ Make #Data the Judge, Not Seniority.

Depersonalize decision-making. In reviews, focus on the metrics: "What did the data from the pilot tell us?" 

This shifts conversations from "I like/dislike this" to "The evidence suggests this," creating a safer environment for intellectual debate.

An experimentation culture doesn't mean chaos. 

It means replacing the fear of failure with the rigor of learning. 

It's how giants learn to dance again.

#Innovation #CorporateCulture

content:
Tech Mahindra Analyst & Advisor Day: From Capabilities to Measurable Outcomes

Tech Mahindra’s #Analyst & #Advisor Day in #Dallas last month underscored a clear strategic posture: a move toward multi-tower modernization (infra, cloud, network, apps, engineering) stitched together by Solutions and large-deals teams — a narrative that positions TechM to compete for larger, outcome-driven transformation mandates.

Three quick #takeaways: 
1️⃣Multi-tower story is the differentiator — not just single-point services.
2️⃣ The event signalled a shift from capability show-and-tell to integrated transformation plays leveraging AI.
3️⃣ Analysts and buyers increasingly want a single, repeatable way to read value — not another capability deck.

What felt different was the intentional stitching of horizontal capabilities into vertical outcomes. Rather than isolated product or service showcases, the program highlighted how infrastructure, cloud and networks are being combined with AI tools, application modernization and solutions to drive enterprise rewiring. The Solutions and large deals teams are increasingly the integrators of that story — which matters because buyers sign on outcomes, not technology stacks.

The leadership and delivery teams I met were pragmatic and focused — the conversations I had with the Specialist Sales leadership were especially constructive. The marketing and analyst engagement efforts also signalled an intent to improve external perception and investor/analyst conviction.
It is clear the directional shift is being driven from the top; what’s emerging now is an opportunity to codify that direction into a single, market-readable articulation.

👉 One focused recommendation:
A practical next step would be to anchor the multi-tower narrative to a repeatable “transformation outcomes” scorecard for analyst engagements. Not a laundry list — rather a single lens that lets the market track whether TechM’s integrated deals are delivering measurable enterprise impact over time. That move elevates the conversation from “we can do these things” to “we deliver these outcomes.”

Appreciate the thoughtful interactions with the teams — in particular Ram Ramachandran Arindam Dutta Roy, Peeyush Dubey Srikrishna C., and Dhiraj A.. Also thanks to the wider analyst relations and marketing teams for a tightly run program. Looking forward to seeing this modernization narrative translate into measurable scale in the coming quarters. 

Tech services leaders or analyst teams wanting to architect outcome-led GTM formats, happy to connect.

content:
Sometimes you walk into certain rooms and you can feel the vantage point.

This was one such room. An eclectic mix of thought leaders from IGIB Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay Accenture Premas Life Sciences Pvt Ltd. Colossa Ventures Adya 

The topic: To create a data commons for Indian omics (genes, etc), collaborate on creating solutions based on the data that will go a long way towards creating new drugs, diagnostics, clinical solutions for Indian diaspora (in India and abroad).

The goal: 
✨ To bring hospitals and organisations that hold Indian data, starting with cancer patients, 

✨ Bring specialists who work on wet lab and genomics and multi-omics solutions to solution on this data

✨ Bring AI specialists onboard to create new BioFMs (bio foundational models) as well as Agentic solutions that will ensure these solutions are easily accessible to the end users

✨ Bring large muti faceted players to bring global capabilities as well as specialists and distribution capabilities 

✨ Bring investors onboard who can help drive the capital raise for such a transformative project.

Auspicious beginnings for one of the most promising developments in India that will eventually help solve key pharma and healthcare challenges in India. 

Some examples

*️⃣ Indian genetic study for cancer drugs has never been done till date. This results in drugs created for Western genes not working in India. Result? We end up using chemo to blast all cells - good and bad, without the ability to target.

*️⃣ Same applies for chronic diseases which also need targeted studies.

The good news? Indian govt gave a grant of Rs 50cr to collect the first 800 samples. This initiative also helped align the major hospitals and paved the way for a future collaboration at far deeper levels.

While such initiatives exist in the US, EU, China - nothing like this exists in India. 

I hold in my heart the hope that this initiative will find its true path and funds to truly add value to the nation in the years to come!

content:
Post 2: The Struggle to Patch a Future That Wasn’t Designed Yet

Through the last quarter of 2024, we kept pushing. We tried harder to stitch things together:

🫸 Vibe coding tools for apps
🫸 Workflow builders for logic
🫸 APIs for LLMs
🫸 Custom scripts for data

We learned a lot about how users actually try to use these systems.

And what we learned was simple:
🛑 It takes a tremendous amount of effort to make these tools behave like one coherent system.
🛑 Every time we added something new, something else broke.
🛑 Data didn’t line up. Schemas didn’t match. Agents lost context. Costs blew up.

We also had a bigger realization. We didn’t want to build just another vertical AI solution.

If you’ve followed my earlier posts, you know why we strongly believe platforms beat vertical point solutions in the long run.

But we also knew this:
You can’t build a true platform by patching together tools that were never designed to be one.

So we stepped back and asked ourselves:
If AI agents are going to do 80–90% of enterprise work in the future…

Why are we still building stacks meant for humans to operate? That was the turning point.

author.name: Shayak Mazumder
author.info: CEO & CTO - Artificial Intelligence, GenAI, Agentic AI, LLMs | Deep tech startups | | Ex-Indian Navy | INSEAD MBA | MD, Rocket Internet | International Business | Global Ecommerce & Supply Chain

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
Post 3: Why We Decided to Build the Adya platform

At some point, we stopped trying to fix what existed. And decided to build what was missing. Vanij was born from that decision.

We asked: what would a platform look like if it were designed from day one for AI-native systems, not just AI-assisted apps?

That’s where our core choices came from:
✨ Agent Studio — so anyone can build real agentic workflows, no-code, by connecting hundreds of LLMs, tools (via MCPs), databases, and AI libraries.
✨ Multi-Agentic Network (MAN) — because centralized orchestration kept breaking when we scaled. We needed agents that could coordinate and run in parallel.
✨ AI-ETL — because without unifying enterprise data, agents are blind.
Model Studio — because enterprises must own and fine-tune their models, not just call APIs.
✨ Structured App Studio — because zero-shot code generation was too fragile for real systems.
✨ Cloud Studio - because enterprises want to host on their own cloud tenants and want the flexibility to shift as needed between cloud providers.

Every one of these came from pain we personally felt while building.

Today, when people ask me how Adya compares to vibe coding tools, I say this:
✅ Vibe coding helps you build apps faster. Adya helps you build systems where AI actually runs the business.

For us, this wasn’t a product decision. It was a survival decision.

And it’s why we believe Adya represents the next stage in the evolution (maybe a revolution) — from tools that write code, to platforms that run intelligence.

author.name: Shayak Mazumder
author.info: CEO & CTO - Artificial Intelligence, GenAI, Agentic AI, LLMs | Deep tech startups | | Ex-Indian Navy | INSEAD MBA | MD, Rocket Internet | International Business | Global Ecommerce & Supply Chain

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
Post 5: The Adya Five-Level Framework

Here’s how Adya is structured:

✨ Level 1 – Core IP
 Multi-agent networks, event memory, adaptive governance.

✨ Level 2 – Platform (Build Layer)
 Model Studio, Agent Studio, App Studio, AI ETL, CPaaS, cloud-agnostic deployment.

✨ Level 3 – ESA (Bridge Layer)
 The Enterprise Strategy Agent — a reusable core orchestration.

✨ Level 4 – Domain Specific ESAs
 Same ESA, tuned for:
✅ Market research
✅ Healthcare & drug discovery
✅ Energy transition
✅ Sales & strategy

✨ Level 5 – Enterprise Configuration
 Each enterprise swaps:
👉 Data
👉 LLMs
👉 Governance
👉 Integrations
👉 Cloud

Same system. Different realities. This is how you get build + buy without compromise.

content:
Post 6: Why This Matters Long-Term (2030 View)

By 2030, enterprises will not run on one SaaS. They’ll have:

✨ Vertical AI tools on the fringe
✨ Hyperscaler primitives at the core
✨ Dozens of internal agents and workflows

What they’ll need is:
✅ A single operating system to orchestrate everything.

Adya is built to be that system.
✨ Not a product.
✨ Not just a platform.
✨ An enterprise operating system for agentic AI — where build and buy finally converge.

content:
This is a rather special partnership. India is the most populous country in the world and we are number two in the world when it comes to the number of engineers. 

Yet, when it comes to AI benchmarks, there is just one Indian company in the top 100 in AI benchmarks. We need to do better. 

We need to focus on creating solutions that will impact the lives of people in and around us to solutions that will become globally successful. 

As AI becomes more powerful, our arbitrage of low-cost engineering talent from India is going to fade. 

That is going to get replaced with high quality solutions that India will offer to the rest of the world.

This is the very moment that India was waiting for, and we should seize it with both hands. Our culture promotes education, focus on creating high-quality solutions.

The last 30 years of China's leadership came through a lot of hard work, deep learnings around infrastructure and manufacturing base, something that China has historically had strength in. 

I believe India historically has been strong when it comes to intellectual prowess and as well as industrial manufacturing.

With the coming of AI, we can empower the nation and we can create a lot of solutions that will really help propel us to global leadership.

With deep gratitude to Shri Sanjiv Singh Shri Sumeet Jarangal Mamatha Venkatesh

Post 3: Distribution Eats Models for Breakfast

The second realization: distribution matters more than model quality.

OpenAI & Google sit inside:
✅ every developer stack,
✅ every cloud marketplace,
✅ every enterprise shortlist.

Their distribution cost → almost zero.

For a company like us:
💰 every customer needs GTM, sales, trust-building.

Even if you’re 10–20% better, it doesn’t matter if you’re 10x harder to adopt.

China built strong local models because the market was insulated. India isn’t.

Here, you compete with Google and OpenAI from day one.

By late 2024, we knew:
 👉 A few % of performance won’t beat global distribution engines.
👇 Then came the real shift: reasoning + agentic models.

#Distribution #GoToMarket #AI #Startups #Adya

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
Post 3: Why “Build” (Platforms) Scare Investors
Pure platforms have the opposite problem of buy solutions.

They promise:
🟢 Infinite flexibility
🟢 Full control
🟢 Deep customization

But investors worry because:
🛑 Time-to-value is slow
🛑 Customers must “start from scratch”
🛑 ROI is hard to show

Platforms feel like dev tools, not business systems. So platforms are seen as:
🫸 High-risk, high-effort, unclear outcome.

This is why investors try to force companies into either build or buy. But that framing is outdated.

Post 4: Adya’s Core Insight – Build vs Buy Is a False Choice

Adya starts from a different belief: In the agentic AI era, enterprises don’t want to either build or buy.

They want a system that lets them do both — on the same foundation.

So Adya is not:
❌ A vertical AI SaaS
❌ A pure build-it-yourself platform

Adya is a five-level system that combines:
✅ The fungibility of a platform
✅ The speed of ready solutions

This is where the real differentiation begins ✨

author.info: CEO & CTO - Artificial Intelligence, GenAI, Agentic AI, LLMs | Deep tech startups | | Ex-Indian Navy | INSEAD MBA | MD, Rocket Internet | International Business | Global Ecommerce & Supply Chain

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
Post 2: Why “Buy” (Vertical AI) Breaks in Enterprise AI

Vertical AI looks attractive on paper:
✅ Invoice AI
✅ Research AI
✅ Sales AI
✅ Healthcare AI

Fast to deploy. Easy to explain. But in enterprise reality, vertical AI fails because:

❌ Data leaves the enterprise tenant
 ❌ LLMs are vendor-controlled
 ❌ Governance is opaque
 ❌ One product = one use case
 ❌ Reuse across domains is almost impossible

Vertical AI works only on the fringe of enterprises. Core systems demand:

🪧 Control
🪧 Compliance
🪧 Hosting choice
🪧 Model choice
🪧 Governance choice

That’s where “buy” alone stops working.

- aziende che rinnegano il cambiamento e che falliranno per mano di chi l'AI la sta usando senza se e senza ma

il nostro business è diverso, è più complesso, ci servono tool custom, non è precisa, non tracciamo il dato, non si può implementare così velocemente etc etc

ci sono molti più imprenditori e manager avversi al cambiamento e ad abbracciare l'AI di quello che a parole sembra.
Quindi immense opportunità per chi, per davvero, la sta usando come leva competitiva.

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
"Ooh, dat is interessant."
"Holy shit, wat is dit?"
"What the f... , waarom hebben wij dit niet?"

Drie stadia. Dezelfde reactie. En ik zie het steeds vaker.

Op de AFAS hackathon.
Bij klantdemo's.
Zelfs bij borrels als ik mijn telefoon pak.

Het moment dat mensen zien wat er mogelijk is met AI.
Niet als chatbot. Niet als hulpmiddeltje.
Maar als je hele workflow.

Ik open mijn terminal. Vraag mijn AI om ff een lead in salesforce te zetten.
Die haalt data uit Salesforce, transcripten uit mijn meetings, checkt mijn agenda.
En levert een compleet briefing op.

In de tijd dat iemand anders Outlook opent.

Dan volgt de stilte.
En dan: "Waarom hebben wij dit niet?"

--> Omdat het er een jaar geleden niet was.
Dit gaat rete snel!

En het mooie is: je hoeft geen developer te zijn.
Je hoeft niet te coderen (ik kan 't ook niet)

Je moet gewoon durven beginnen.

Met je eigen irritaties. Je eigen workflow. Je eigen data.

En dan kijken wat er gebeurt.

Wanneer was jouw "what the f..." moment met AI?

content:
Afgelopen week zag ik een TikTok voorbij komen dat helemaal viral is gegaan.

De vriendin van YouTuber Gio (Lynn Hermanussen) vroeg serieus aan ChatGPT of er oorlog was, terwijl de straaljagers en bommenwerpers hun om de oren vlogen. 

Niet omdat ze een grap maakte, maar omdat AI voor veel mensen inmiddels voelt als een soort alles-weter.

En laten we eerlijk zijn: AI is een geweldige tool.

Ik gebruik het zelf ook.
Om informatie samen te vatten
Om teksten beter te formatteren
Om mockups te maken voor nieuwe illusie-ontwerpen
Om processen in mijn business te automatiseren

Het kan ongelooflijk veel.

Maar juist daarom denk ik dat er iets nóg belangrijker wordt.

Menselijkheid.
Imperfectie.
Eerlijkheid.
Zelf creëren in plaats van alleen genereren.

Je ziet nu al AI-muziek, AI-influencers, AI-content.

Technisch indrukwekkend dat het kan. 

Maar uiteindelijk zoeken we als mensen altijd connectie met andere mensen.

Een liedje raakt je niet omdat het perfect geproduceerd is.
Het raakt je omdat er een mens achter zit.

Dus ja: gebruik AI.
Zet het op een slimme manier in

Maar zorg dat je eigen stem hoorbaar blijft.

Want technologie kan veel.
Maar authenticiteit blijft onvervangbaar.

Binnen onze eigen Academie bieden we medewerkers een breed aanbod aan trainingen en workshops. Recent stond de training AI in het onderwijs op het programma en dus vroegen we trainer én AI-expert Ivo Wouters gelijk wat tips hoe je met hulp van AI jouw onderwijspraktijk kan verrijken. 

Van de praktische VROT methode tot het geheim achter goed prompten: je ontdekt hoe je met een paar simpele handigheden veel tijd kunt besparen én optimaal gebruik maakt van de nieuwste ontwikkelingen. Ook vertelt onze bestuurder Karin Aarden-Hermsen hoe de stichting kijkt naar generatieve AI. Kortom: het kijken waard!

L’intelligenza artificiale sta cambiando il mercato del lavoro.

Ma il cambiamento più profondo riguarda l’accesso al lavoro.
Sempre più aziende cercano competenze digitali avanzate anche nei ruoli iniziali.

Il risultato è la nascita di una nuova gerarchia delle competenze.

Chi sa utilizzare e comprendere l’IA parte con un vantaggio competitivo.
Chi non ha accesso alla formazione digitale rischia di restare indietro.

content:
"Waarom doen we dit eigenlijk zo?"

Die vraag stelde ik vorige week. De stilte die volgde zei genoeg.

Men denkt nog steeds dat AI wijds geaccepteerd en geimplementeerd wordt in bedrijfsprocessen. Maar dat is niet zo. De markt is nog steeds terughoudend omdat ze niet weten hoe ze AI echt kunnen inzetten voor hun eigen gewin.

Hier is wat ik heb in de afgelopen maand heb geleerd: In 9 minuten een app bouwen is mogelijk, maar als je echt waarde wil toevoegen moet je meer leveren. En dat kost tijd.

Mijn advies: begin niet bij de oplossing. Begin bij de juiste vraag. De bedrijven die doorbreken zijn niet slimmer. Ze stellen betere vragen.

Wat is de laatste keer dat jij een aanname in je werk hebt uitgedaagd?

#AI #Automation

postedAt.postedAgoText: 13 hours ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
Tech tsunami. Is ai de tweede golf?

(En hoe hoog istie?)

Niets is zo uitgesproken als het gebruik van AI tegenwoordig. Van vermeende succesverhalen zoals Spotify die code en functies zogenaamd sneller uitrolt dan het licht met behulp van AI, tot concurrenten die elkaar het leven zuur maken door te proberen de werking van de ander te achterhalen door grote hoeveelheden zogenoemde ‘lokprompts’ te sturen om de chatbot te verleiden de interne werking bloot te geven.

Maar wat werkt nu wel en wat niet?

Zelfverklaarde AI experts buitelen over elkaar heen om hun bewierookte werkwijze aan te prijzen. Met AI lijkt ineens iedereen een expert. Ravensburger: iedereen een artiest.

Ik kan net zo min in de toekomst kijken als ieder ander, maar heb toch het vermoeden dat het overlaten van het bouwen van bedrijfskritische software aan AI wachten is op de eerste grote instorting van een toonaangevende organisatie.

Zie het even als een technologische tsunami waarvan we de tweede golf nog niet zagen aankomen.

Zoals ik het zie: de eerste golf was data die, nadat het internet steeds meer gemeengoed werd, in grote hoeveelheden bij allerlei datalekken in handen kwam van marketeers en mensen met nog minder goede bedoelingen.

De parallel tussen die twee: we waden, en zijn, denk ik, niet voorbereid op wat er nog gaat komen.

Ondertussen loopt mijn eigen brein over van de mogelijkheden die de nieuwe technologie kan bieden.
Wat is zinnig om toe te passen en wat niet? Het begin van iets totaal nieuws. Wat gaat de basis worden van een nieuwe technische stapel die in de toekomst bruikbaar zal zijn? Misschien moet het stof eerst nog wat verder neerdalen voordat daar een zinnig antwoord op te geven is.

Technologie komt van ver.
Van programmeren in assembleertaal tot de opkomst van het allesbepalende JavaScript. Het probleem, als ik voor mezelf spreek, is dat de technologische ontwikkeling de laatste jaren zo snel gaat dat erin investeren op een gegeven moment ook een beetje zinloos begint te voelen. Met andere woorden: er is nu nog te veel aanbod waarvan ik niet precies weet of het morgen nog een relevante keuze is.

En hoeveel concurrenten komen daar in korte tijd nog bij. Keuzestress..

Dus in de tussentijd kijk ik vooral naar wat er gebeurt in de markt. Wat komt er bovendrijven en blijkt op de termijn een relevante optie te zijn?

Ga ik de urenapp die ik ontwikkel steeds meer bouwen met behulp van Ai?  Zodat ik een stuk diep begrip van de interne werking uit handen ga geven? Of ga ik nadenken over functies die ik kan toevoegen die AI gebruiken.

Voorlopig kijk ik het nog even aan.

Photo by Possessed Photography on unsplash
